approaches_tried.approach: Analyze individual sorries for quick wins
approaches_tried.reason: Case B1 sorries require iteration, Case B2 sorries may also need restructuring

key_findings: Root cause: When M' is reflexive (Case B1), non-strict density returns W = M' or W ~ M'
W ~ M' collapses in the quotient, so W is not strictly between M and M'
The current proof uses exfalso in cases that are NOT contradictions
Pattern C iteration is required: if W is not strict, use seriality to escape and recurse
CantorApplication sorries depend on DensityFrameCondition strict density

blocking_issues.description: Current proof attempts exfalso in non-contradictory cases
blocking_issues.impact: Cannot close 6 sorries in Case B1 without restructuring

blocking_issues.description: density_frame_condition_strict gives W not in timeline, dense_timeline_has_intermediate gives non-strict c

next_steps: Restructure proof to use iteration instead of direct case analysis
